Title: Emerging roles for conjugated sterols in plants

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Proposed to contribute to residual steryl glycoside/acyl steryl glycoside synthesis in ugt80A2 ugt80B1 mutants; essential for cell-type differentiation and organogenesis.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant characterization; in vitro glycosylation evidence cited in review
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. At2g19880 is the only Arabidopsis gene encoding glucosylceramide synthase. 2. The enzyme has been shown in vitro to glycosylate sterols, suggesting a possible auxiliary role in steryl glycoside biosynthesis. 3. Null mutants are seedling lethal, preventing direct in vivo assessment of its contribution to conjugated sterol metabolism.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a summarized core gene in the review because it is specifically discussed as a candidate enzyme for glycosylated sterol biosynthesis, but its role in steryl glycoside production in vivo is not established.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: chs1 chilling-sensitive mutant correlates with increased steryl ester levels, but causality is unresolved.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. The chilling-sensitive chs1 mutant shows elevated steryl ester levels. 2. CHS1 encodes a TIR-NB type defense-related protein. 3. The review notes that it remains unclear whether steryl ester accumulation is a direct consequence of CHS1 mutation or a secondary response to chilling injury.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Background/core contextual gene for stress-linked conjugated sterol phenotype; not a direct conjugated sterol metabolic enzyme.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: conjugated sterol biosynthesis; steryl ester biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: steryl ester formation from free sterols
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: free sterols/sterol intermediates; phosphatidylethanolamine or other phospholipids as acyl donor
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers a fatty acyl group from phospholipids, preferentially phosphatidylethanolamine, to free sterols or sterol intermediates to form steryl esters.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: major control point for steryl ester synthesis and free sterol homeostasis in Arabidopsis
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Spherical structures of unknown identity; not apparently lipid droplets
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: seeds; leaves; phloem sap; pollen-associated tissues; senescent leaves
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes steryl ester biosynthesis and maintains free sterol homeostasis; loss causes strong steryl ester reduction and early leaf senescence.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression with microsomal enzyme assays; T-DNA mutants; feeding experiments with mevalonolactone and squalene; sterol profiling; GFP localization
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: senescence; precursor feeding with mevalonolactone or squalene; pathogen interaction context noted
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to modulate steryl ester pools, sterol homeostasis, senescence, and possibly pathogen responses, but manipulation may alter other conjugated sterol classes.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PSAT1 encodes a phospholipid:sterol acyltransferase responsible for a major fraction of steryl ester biosynthesis in Arabidopsis. 2. psat1 mutants show strong steryl ester depletion, hypersensitivity to sterol-pathway precursor feeding, and early leaf senescence. 3. The enzyme may act as a free-sterol homeostasis sensor because its activity is stimulated by sterol end products.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review notes possible allosteric activation by sterol end products; also linked to resistance to Phytophthora infestans when impaired, though associated changes in SG/ASG complicate interpretation.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: conjugated sterol biosynthesis; steryl ester biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: steryl ester formation from free sterols
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: cycloartenol and other sterols; acyl-CoA as acyl donor
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers a fatty acyl group from acyl-CoA to sterols, with preference for cycloartenol, generating steryl esters.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: not established; contributes to steryl ester biosynthesis but appears less decisive than PSAT1 in Arabidopsis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to steryl ester biosynthesis, particularly cycloartenol ester formation; overexpression in seeds increases steryl esters and total sterols.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast complementation in are1 are2 mutant; transgenic overexpression in Arabidopsis seeds; sterol profiling; Arabidopsis T-DNA mutant analysis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ASAT1 is the only tested Arabidopsis MBOAT family member able to restore steryl ester biosynthesis in yeast. 2. The enzyme prefers cycloartenol as acyl acceptor and overexpression elevates seed steryl ester and total sterol content. 3. Genetic analyses indicate ASAT1 contributes to steryl ester metabolism but has a smaller role than PSAT1 in sterol homeostasis.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Its exact physiological contribution remains uncertain because asat1 mutants show weaker phenotypes than psat1 mutants, and flowers retain near-wild-type steryl ester levels.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: conjugated sterol biosynthesis; steryl glycoside biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: free sterols such as sitosterol and stigmasterol; UDP-glucose as sugar donor
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose from UDP-glucose to the C3-hydroxyl group of sterols to form steryl glycosides.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: major contributor to seed steryl glycoside synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Differentially expressed across organs/tissues; transcript levels rapidly down-regulated after seed imbibition
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Major determinant of seed steryl glycoside accumulation; mutants reduce SG content and modestly reduce seed size/root elongation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA mutant characterization; sterol profiling; recombinant enzyme biochemical assays
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT80A2 encodes a sterol glycosyltransferase using UDP-glucose to produce steryl glycosides. 2. It is the major contributor to steryl glycoside accumulation in seeds, especially β-sitosteryl and stigmasteryl glycosides. 3. Mutants show strong reduction of SG+ASG in aerial tissues and reduced seed/root growth phenotypes.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Residual SG/ASG in the double mutant indicates additional enzymes may contribute.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: conjugated sterol biosynthesis; steryl glycoside biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: free sterols, with preference linked to brassicasterol; UDP-glucose as sugar donor
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose from UDP-glucose to sterols, producing steryl glycosides with substrate selectivity distinct from UGT80A2.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: specialized contributor rather than overall major control step
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Differentially expressed across organs/tissues; transcript levels rapidly down-regulated after seed imbibition
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for normal seed coat and seed development traits and contributes with UGT80A2 to glycosylated sterol production and pollen coat maturation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA mutant characterization; double-mutant analysis; sterol profiling; recombinant enzyme biochemical assays
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT80B1 is a sterol glycosyltransferase with substrate preferences distinct from UGT80A2. 2. Mutation causes transparent testa (tt15), reduced seed size, root elongation defects, and contributes to pollen coat maturation defects in the double mutant. 3. Seed phenotypes occur despite limited changes in total SG/ASG, implying specialized sterol glycoside composition effects.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The review highlights that UGT80B1 may synthesize a specialized or minor sterol glycoside important for seed development.

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Arabidopsis thaliana; Nicotiana tabacum; Withania somnifera
Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: sterol glycosyltransferase-like stress-responsive metabolic regulator discussed via transgenic overexpression
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: steryl glycosides; acyl steryl glycosides; possibly withanolide glycosides context in W. somnifera
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: high temperature; low temperature; methyl jasmonate; salicylic acid
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: glycosylation of sterols associated with stress adaptation
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: stress-responsive induction and increased conjugated sterol formation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression enhances salt, heat, and cold tolerance and can increase insect resistance; downregulation increases susceptibility to heat and pathogens.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic overexpression in Arabidopsis, tobacco, and W. somnifera; gene downregulation in W. somnifera; stress assays
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. WsSGTL1 is induced by temperature and defense-related signals and is associated with stress-responsive sterol glycosylation. 2. Overexpression confers improved tolerance to salt, heat, and cold in heterologous and native systems. 3. In W. somnifera, altered biotic stress outcomes may also reflect changes in withanosides, so direct attribution to SG/ASG alone is uncertain.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although enzymatically a pathway gene, this review emphasizes its stress-inducible role across species; included in Regulation_Genes to capture the paper’s framing of its stress-responsive control function.
